name: Stuffed Peppers
recipeIngredient: 1 egg white or egg substitute (we use Ener-g)
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse quinoa well and set aside to drain.
2. Chop onion, garlic, and sundried tomatoes and set aside.
3. Cut tops off of bell peppers, rinse the bodies clear of seeds, and trim the seed pod from the tops. Save the tops.
4. Heat 2 cups of broth to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan. Once boiling, add quinoa and sundried tomatoes. Cover and bring to simmer for 12 minutes.
5. Add buckwheat groats to 1 egg white (or egg substitute) and mix until groats are coated. Turn mixture into a dry frying pan and cook over medium heat for 2-3 minutes, stirring well to keep groats separated. After 2-3 minutes, add 1½ cups of broth, onion, garlic, and other spices (you might want to mix the spices with the tomato sauce in the next step instead). Cover and bring to simmer for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. Drain quinoa from the stove, saving vegetable stock. Place quinoa in a bowl and add buckwheat groats once they are done. Add 1 cup of tomato puree or your favorite tomato sauce and mix well. Spoon generously into bell peppers and replace the tops.
7. Pour leftover broth into a large pot with a steamer tray. Add enough broth or water to reach just below the bottom of the steamer rack (½ - ¾ inch / .5 - 3cm is sufficient) and place peppers in the pot. Secure lid and place on stove. Bring to boil, then let simmer for 20-30 minutes until peppers are very soft. Serve.
This dish tastes even better the next day!
